quote_of_day.text:
>Be me
>UC Bengali Chad
>pro-Shudra pro-chamar
>Join Iskcon
>have 4 girlfriends from 4 different castes
>they like me so much
Why my life so easy?
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of caste tension persisted over the board, with a dominant front of antagonism towards AI emerging in the afternoon.

A scattered drizzle of claims and counter-claims about romantic conquests across caste lines fell in the morning, with one anon asserting they had four girlfriends from different castes.

A cold snap of ethnic slurs moved through the North-South divide discussion, with north Indians and south Indians trading insults.

Meanwhile, a low-pressure system of techno-optimism flared up as an anon claimed AI had 'raped' certain caste groups, specifically JEE Bhangis and code Chamars.

articles.title: Anon Claims AI Won't Impact Jaab Market
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread claiming that motorized vehicles would not eliminate traditional horse-based transportation, but rather transform it, and made a similar assertion about AI not impacting the jaab market. Other anons responded with skepticism.

One anon noted that AI would primarily enhance ISR capabilities and unmanned land-based platforms, but humanoid robots would not replace humans due to limitations in competing with human abilities.

Another anon expressed concern that automation would lead to widespread job losses, making only military personnel and sarkari admin jobs safe. The discussion turned to the economic implications of automation.

An anon argued that if a nation could produce enough to feed its people without anyone working, the value of money and labor would change, and people could receive the same food without working.

However, another anon countered that without jobs, people would not be able to pay taxes, and the government would not have funds to pay its employees or military personnel.

Some anons also discussed the potential for robo-soldiers and the impact of elites on the job market. Ex Jeefag mentioned that robo-soldiers already exist, and one anon wondered if elites might decide to get rid of humans.

Another anon joked that with nukes, real war has become obsolete. The thread also included some humorous exchanges.

An anon shared a meme about jhaatu meme exam bhangis having big egos, while another anon simply posted 'kek yaar' and 'kek'.

The conversation concluded with an anon questioning how the country would obtain crude oil and fertilizers if automation replaced human labor. Another anon stated that it is already decided that elites will merge with AI at a neural level.

articles.title: Caste and Marriage Preferences
articles.body: Anon opened the discussion with a brief statement: 'No mishra jiii not with mullarinooo.' One respondent reacted with a question about leaving someone referred to as 'musal chad' and marrying a man described as 'dysgenic pot potbellied ponga.' This anon concluded with a definitive 'Never.' Another poster referenced the OP directly, questioning whether individuals with surnames like Sharma and Mishra who express such views are 'self-haters.' A third anon addressed the OP with an insult directed at someone's mother, using a Hindi phrase.

articles.title: North-South Divide: Caste and Ethnic Slurs Fly
articles.body:
Anon sparked controversy with a post calling out 'northies' for using the term 'abbo' to refer to people from southern India, while allegedly living near people they consider inferior.

Anon claimed that 'Tambrahm' Brahmins from the north moved to Tamil Nadu when invited by local kings and were given land.

Another anon countered with a post labeling Tamils as 'scums of the pajeet race' and referencing a godfather figure, Periyar, who allegedly kicked out these Brahmins.

Anon then brought up the topic of the Indian intelligence agency, R&AW, with one poster questioning the presence of 'kundigan' officers, calling them 'waste of oxygen.' A respondent pushed back, stating that many Tamils and Malayalis were, in fact, part of R&AW.

The conversation took a turn for the absurd with discussions of beauty and genetics. Anon described women from one region as 'very Aryan looking,' while another poster joked about the physical appearance of people from other areas.

The thread was also marked by the use of derogatory terms for people from various regions, including 'dumeel,' 'kundigan,' and 'gobar.' Anon questioned the existence of a Tamil equivalent to a supposed north Indian term, leading to a dismissive response.

The discussion touched on historical and cultural differences between regions, with one poster mentioning 'Kannadiga chads' and 'Mallu femboys.' The thread remained focused on exchanges of claims and insults rather than constructive dialogue.

articles.title: Anon claims to have 4 girlfriends from different castes
articles.body: Anon posted a story about being a UC Bengali, pro-Shudra, pro-chamar, and a member of Iskcon, with four girlfriends from different castes. They wondered why their life was so easy. Another anon responded that Iskon is 'the scummiest place on earth' and 'a stain on Hindu religion.' Anon also received comments about their claim, with one saying they are in the 'friend zone' and another calling them 'ponganon.'

articles.title: Anon Claims AI Has 'Raped' JEE Bhangis and Code Chamars
articles.body:
Anon posted that AI has 'raped' JEE Bhangis and code chamars, claiming they used to mock AI on plebbit but are now in 'COPE mode.' The poster listed supposed recent claims from these groups, including that AI will only replace mediocre software developers and that core branches will rise.

Another anon replied that AI has also impacted their job opportunities, citing AI's capabilities in graphic design. A different anon shared links to articles on the AI revolution, suggesting they remain relevant.

Anon also joked that rich people are '80 iq chimps' who will get rich and powerful while others are critical of AI. One respondent called the discussion 'cope' and accused others of not reading the shared articles.
